annotations.label: Identity function

anchor.snippet: is a function that always returns the value that was used as its argument

annotations.note: The identity function is Lean core `id` (returns its argument unchanged), used pervasively throughout Mathlib (e.g. `Function.bijective_id`).

annotations.label: Formal definition of identity function

anchor.snippet: is defined to be a function with

annotations.note: Lean core `id : α → α` with `id_eq : id a = a` exactly captures f(X)=X with equal domain and codomain.

annotations.label: Identity function is bijective

anchor.snippet: is clearly an injective function as well as a surjective function

annotations.note: `Function.bijective_id : Bijective (@id α)` (built from `injective_id` and `surjective_id`) states exactly that the identity is bijective.

annotations.label: Notation for identity function
annotations.note: Mathlib uses `id` (and `𝟙` for identity morphisms, `1` in `Function.End`) as notation for the identity function.

annotations.label: Identity function as identity relation

anchor.snippet: is given by the identity relation

annotations.note: `Set.diagonal α := {p | p.1 = p.2}` formalizes the identity relation, but no lemma directly equates the graph of `id` with it.

annotations.label: Composition with identity function
annotations.note: `Function.comp_id : f ∘ id = f` and `Function.id_comp : id ∘ f = f` (core, tagged `mfld_simps` in Mathlib) state composition with the identity exactly.

annotations.label: Identity is identity element of function monoid

anchor.snippet: is the identity element of the monoid of all functions from

annotations.note: The `Monoid (Function.End α)` instance defines `one := id` and `mul := (· ∘ ·)`, making `id` the identity element of the endomorphism monoid.

annotations.label: Identity as monoid identity element / identity morphism

anchor.snippet: one can alternately define the identity function on

annotations.note: `CategoryTheory.CategoryStruct.id` (notation `𝟙 X`) generalizes the identity function to the identity morphism in any category.

annotations.label: Identity function is a linear operator

anchor.snippet: is a linear operator when applied to vector spaces

annotations.note: `LinearMap.id : M →ₗ[R] M` packages the identity function as a linear map, formalizing that it is a linear operator.

annotations.label: Identity function represented by identity matrix

anchor.snippet: is represented by the identity matrix

annotations.note: `LinearMap.toMatrix_id : LinearMap.toMatrix v₁ v₁ id = 1` states the identity map's matrix is the identity matrix.

annotations.label: Identity is completely multiplicative
annotations.note: `isMultiplicative_id` proves the arithmetic function `ArithmeticFunction.id` is multiplicative (coprime version), but Mathlib has no `CompletelyMultiplicative` predicate so the full claim is not directly stated.

annotations.label: Identity function is an isometry
annotations.note: `isometry_id : Isometry (id : α → α)` states the identity function is an isometry.

annotations.label: Identity function is continuous
annotations.note: `continuous_id : Continuous (id : X → X)` states the identity function is continuous.

annotations.label: Identity function is idempotent
annotations.note: `IsIdempotentElem.one` shows `1` is idempotent in any monoid, which gives idempotence of `id = 1` in `Function.End`; also `comp_id` trivially gives `id ∘ id = id`, but no decl literally labels `id` idempotent.

annotations.label: Maps from a singleton set

anchor.snippet: Every map from a set of a single element to itself is necessarily the identity map

annotations.note: `Subsingleton.elim` (with `funext`) makes any self-map on a subsingleton equal to `id`, but there is no dedicated lemma stating this for singleton-type endofunctions.
